answer: OpenAI was founded in 2015 as a nonprofit research lab. It aims to ensure artificial general intelligence benefits all of humanity. The organization is known for developing AI tools like ChatGPT.

results.title: The Story of OpenAI | Medium
results.snippet: In December **2015**, OpenAI was founded as a non-profit by Sam Altman, Elon Musk, Ilya Sutskever, Greg Brockman, Trevor Blackwell, Vicki Cheung, Andrej Karpathy, Durk Kingma, John Schulman, Pamela Vagata, and Wojciech Zaremba. After leaving OpenAI, Sutskever — like three other original co-founders — started his own AI company, Safe SuperIntelligence. OpenAI’s breakthroughs are grounded in transformer technology — the architecture introduced by _Attention Is All You Need_, which enables models to weigh the importance of input data. OpenAI’s ultimate goal is **Artificial General Intelligence (AGI)** — AI that can outperform humans at most valuable tasks. OpenAI’s journey — from a small nonprofit to a global force reshaping how we work, study and create — is one of the most impactful stories of our time. As OpenAI improves its technology, navigates safety questions, and continues toward its vision of AGI, one thing remains clear: **the future of AI is not just about flashy technology — it’s about how it benefits society.**.

In 2024, OpenAI partnered with [Arizona State University](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Arizona-State-University), [Reddit,](https://www.britannica.com/money/Reddit)[News Corp](https://www.britannica.com/money/News-Corporation), and [Apple](https://www.britannica.com/money/Apple-Inc), acquired Multi and Chat.com, and released SearchGPT, GPT-4, OpenAI o1, and ChatGPT Pro. OpenAI is an American [artificial intelligence](https://www.britannica.com/technology/artificial-intelligence) company known for developing ChatGPT, DALL-E, and Whisper, tools that helped spark the global boom in generative AI.
